text:
Here are the results:

**8 merged pull requests** close a Linear issue whose state is **not `done`**:

| PR # | Repo | Linear Issue | Issue State |
|------|------|-------------|-------------|
| 1002 | acme/web | ENG-136 | canceled |
| 1003 | acme/web | ENG-103 | backlog |
| 1010 | acme/web | ENG-151 | in_progress |
| 1017 | acme/web | ENG-117 | canceled |
| 1025 | acme/web | ENG-135 | in_review |
| 1030 | acme/web | ENG-124 | in_progress |
| 1033 | acme/web | ENG-147 | canceled |
| 1037 | acme/web | ENG-102 | in_progress |

**Total: 8**

Of these, 4 Linear issues are **canceled**, 3 are **in_progress**, 1 is **in_review**, and 1 is **backlog** — none are `done`. All 8 PRs are from the `acme/web` repo. These appear to be cases where the work was merged but the linked Linear issue was never transitioned to done (or was canceled).
I'll start by getting all merged pull requests that have a linked Linear issue, then check each Linear issue's state.

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"
(def merged-prs (filter #(and (= (:state %) "merged") (:closes_linear %))
                        (github__list_pull_requests {:state "merged"})))

(def linear-ids (distinct (map :closes_linear merged-prs)))

(def linear-issues (map linear__get_issue (map #(hash-map :id %) linear-ids)))

(def issue-states (into {} (map (fn [i] [(:id i) (:state i)]) linear-issues)))

(def re…(+276)

message: unknown symbol 'hash-map'. Run (tables) to list your capabilities and (describe :name) for one of them.

text: Let me fix the map construction:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"
(def linear-issues (map (fn [lid] (linear__get_issue {:id lid})) linear-ids))
(def issue-states (into {} (map (fn [i] [(:id i) (:state i)]) linear-issues)))
"}

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"
(def results
  (filter (fn [pr]
            (let [lid (:closes_linear pr)
                  state (get issue-states lid)]
              (and state (not= state "done"))))
          merged-prs))

(def output
  (map (fn [pr] {:pr (:number pr) :linear_issue (:closes_linear pr) :issue_state (get issue-states (:closes_linear pr))}) results))

(def total (count output))
"}
